top_signals.title: Europe gas scale three-month highs as Middle East war risks tighten LNG supply
top_signals.summary: European natural gas prices surged to their highest levels in more than three months on Wednesday, as an intensifying U.S.-Iran conflict sparked anxieties over a sweeping disruption to global liquefied natural gas (LNG) supplies. The benchmark front-month Dutch gas contract rose 3% to hit 54.40 euro per megawatt-hour (MWh), marking its highest trading level since ...

top_signals.title: Five Ways China Is Shielding Itself as the Hormuz Cease-Fire Collapses
top_signals.summary: The U.S.-Iran cease-fire that was supposed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz for good is falling apart.  American forces reimposed a naval blockade on Iranian ports this week and hit dozens of targets along Iran's coast, and Tehran answered by striking tankers moving through the strait without its permission.  Brent crude, which had drifted down into the mid-$70s while the June peace deal held, jumped back above $85 a barrel on the news, its highest level since the truce was signed.  It's the second time this year the market has had…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Fed’s Goolsbee calls June inflation data encouraging but cautious
top_signals.summary: Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee said Tuesday that consumer price inflation data for June showed encouraging signs but cautioned that one month of data is not sufficient to determine if inflation is returning to the 2% target. “I would be feeling a lot better” if there were several months of such data, Goolsbee told ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: US Producer Prices Record Surprise Decline
top_signals.summary: Producer prices in the US declined 0.3% mom in June 2026, following a downwardly revised 0.6% rise in May and below forecasts of a flat reading. It is the first decrease in PPI since August 2025 and the largest since April last year. Prices of goods went down 1.4%, the most since July 2022, led ...

top_signals.summary: The firm seeks to offer tokenized long-term investments like "Treasury funds, iShares ETFs, and even private markets."

top_signals.title: Trump rescinds 20% fee for Hormuz transits, replaces with US investments by Gulf states
top_signals.summary: The US will not charge a 20% reimbursement fee for all volumes transiting the Strait of Hormuz but will instead make trade and investment deals with Middle East nations, US President Donald Trump said in a social media post on Tuesday. The post came a day after Trump suggested the 20% reimbursement fee in a ...

top_signals.title: Wärtsilä agreement to strengthen operational reliability and maintenance predictability for Pardus Energy FSRU
top_signals.summary: Technology group Wärtsilä has signed a five-year Lifecycle Agreement with Ireland-based Pardus Energy to support the reliable and predictable operation of the company’s Floating Storage and Regasification Unit (FSRU) vessel, ‘TURQUOISE P’. The agreement will provide dedicated contract management, maintenance planning and spare parts to help Pardus Energy improve maintenance readiness, strengthen cost predictability and prepare for ...

alerts.title: DP World Advances Jeddah Terminal Transformation With 35 New Electric Tractors
alerts.summary: DP World has deployed 35 Electric Terminal Tractors (ETTs) at Jeddah Islamic Port’s South Container Terminal (SCT). The deployment expands DP World’s terminal tractor fleet by over 20% and advances its $800 million long-term investment in smarter, more resilient and lower-carbon port infrastructure at one of Saudi Arabia’s busiest container gateways. This is expected to ...

alerts.title: Shabana Mahmood set to be named Chancellor by Burnham
alerts.summary: Shabana Mahmood is set to be named Chancellor by Andy Burnham next week when the former Manchester mayor takes the keys to Number 10, according to reports.  The home secretary has now pipped Ed Miliband, Yvette Cooper and Wes Streeting to Rachel Reeves’ post, the Financial Times reported.  A source told the paper: “Shabana is [...]
